title: rubus graniticola
intro: Rubus graniticola, also known as the Granite Bramble or Granite Raspberry, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) native to Western Australia. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which may help to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus graniticola include its potential to boost the immune system, improve circulation, and provide antioxidant protection. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, tinctures, and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although its availability and usage may be limited due to its endangered status.
